data.title: Vor Biopharma: Strategic Autoimmune Pivot and Telitacicept’s >$2B gMG/SjD Opportunity Underpins Buy Rating
data.description:
Farzin Haque, an analyst from Jefferies, has initiated a new Buy rating on Vor Biopharma (VOR).

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Farzin Haque has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Vor Biopharma’s strategic pivot and telitacicept’s profile. He highlights that the company’s move into autoimmune diseases, anchored by a global (ex-Greater China) license for telitacicept, taps a large and growing gMG market in the U.S., where the drug has already shown strong, placebo-adjusted improvements in China that compare favorably with current and late-stage competitors.

Farzin Haque’s rating is based on the belief that dual BAFF/APRIL inhibition could offer differentiated, durable efficacy and a more suitable long‑term safety profile than many existing chronic therapies, while addressing all key pathogenic antibody classes. He also emphasizes the substantial upside from primary Sjögren’s disease, an area with no approved disease‑modifying systemic treatments, supported by encouraging Phase III data from China, a solid cash position through major catalysts, and a valuation that does not yet fully reflect the >$2B combined commercial potential in gMG and SjD.

According to TipRanks, Haque is a 2-star analyst with an average return of 2.9% and a 60.00% success rate.

In another report released on March 19, Wells Fargo also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a $30.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: ONGC: Compelling Buy on Mispriced Oil Outlook, Robust Cash Flows, and Emerging Production Catalysts
data.description:
Analyst Mayank Maheshwari of Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on Oil & Natural Gas Corp. Ltd., with a price target of INR363.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Mayank Maheshwari has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to both valuation and improving fundamentals at ONGC. He argues that the market is still valuing the stock as if Brent crude will remain around US$60 per barrel, even though supply‑demand conditions point to a structurally tighter oil market beyond the current Middle East conflict.

Maheshwari also highlights ONGC’s strong free cash flow yield of more than 18% at an assumed long‑term Brent price of US$80, which he sees as compelling. In addition, he points to catalysts such as rising production from the Mumbai High field, higher realizations for domestic natural gas, and better profitability from downstream refinery subsidiaries and associates once geopolitical tensions ease, all while the stock trades at only about 6x one‑year forward earnings.

In another report released today, TipRanks – Anthropic also initiated coverage with a Buy rating on the stock with a INR319.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Nokia: Improving Fundamentals and AI-Driven Network Upside Offset by Valuation Premium, Justifying a Hold Rating
data.description:
Goldman Sachs analyst Alexander Duval upgraded the rating on Nokia to a Hold on March 27, setting a price target of €8.00.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Alexander Duval has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, notably a more optimistic outlook for Nokia’s Optical and IP Networks businesses, which are set to benefit from sustained AI infrastructure investment and strong recent order momentum, including sizable backlog from AI and cloud clients. However, he also notes that Radio Networks remains structurally challenged by a stagnant wireless RAN market, even if it now represents a smaller share of group revenue.

At the same time, Nokia has reset profitability expectations, giving investors greater confidence that margins have a firm floor, and the company’s fundamentals appear to be improving. Yet Duval judges that the current valuation already discounts much of this progress, with Nokia trading at a premium to the broader European tech sector versus a historical discount, leading him to see limited relative upside and thus support only a Neutral stance rather than a more positive rating.

According to TipRanks, Duval is a 4-star analyst with an average return of 7.6% and a 55.86% success rate. Duval covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Nokia, Infineon Technologies AG, and Logitech.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
